title: CET-6 Vocabulary Journal - Day 2
content: This post continues my CET-6 vocabulary journal. Day 2 focuses on higher-level words that often appear in serious discussions, such as politics, education and social issues, as well as some useful vocabulary for academic writing. IntroductionFor Day 2, I focus on two groups of language: core vocabulary about behavior, attitudes and complexity (for example: bribe, intricate, ambivalent, tolerant). academic writing words and phrases that help me sound more formal and precise in essays (for example: substantiate, exacerbate, give rise to). For each item I include: a concise English definition a few synonyms at least one example sentence, followed by its meaning in Chinese when needed Word list for Day 21. bribe (n.v.)Meaning: money or something valuable given to someone to persuade them to do something dishonest or illegal; to give such money or gifts. In simple terms, a bribe is a secret “payment” to make someone break the rules. Example 1: The official was accused of accepting a large bribe from the company. Example 2 (optional): They tried to bribe the guard to let them enter the building. 2. intricate (adj.)Meaning: having many small parts or details that are closely arranged in a complicated way. An intricate pattern or system is beautiful or impressive, but it can be hard to understand at first. Example 1: The artist spent hours working on the intricate pattern on the vase. Example 2 (optional): The novel has an intricate plot that keeps readers guessing until the end. 3. incite (v.)Meaning: to encourage or stir up strong, often violent or unlawful behavior or feelings in other people. When you incite someone, you push them to take action, usually in a dangerous or aggressive way. Example 1: the offense of inciting racial hatred Example 2 (optional): The speech was criticized for attempting to incite violence. 4. trivial (adj.)Meaning: not important or serious; having little value or significance. Trivial things are so small that they are not worth spending too much time or energy on. Example 1: We shouldn’t waste time arguing about such trivial matters. Example 2 (optional): At first the problem seemed trivial, but later it caused serious delays. 5. adept adapt adopt (adj.v.)Meaning: three similar-looking words with different uses: adept (adj.): very skilled or good at doing something. adapt (v.): to change something or yourself so that it works better in a new situation. adopt (v.): to take something and start using it as your own (for example, a child, an idea or a method). In short: you are adept at a skill, you adapt to a new situation, and you adopt a new idea or way of doing things. Example 1: She is adept at explaining intricate concepts in a simple way. Example 2 (optional): Many universities had to adapt quickly to online teaching and adopt new digital platforms during the pandemic. 6. ambivalent (adj.)Meaning: having mixed or conflicting feelings about someone or something at the same time. Synonyms: torn, uncertain, conflicted. Example 1: She felt ambivalent about studying abroad, excited about new opportunities but worried about leaving her family. 中文：她对出国留学感到很矛盾，一方面为新的机会感到兴奋，另一方面又担心离开家人。 7. subjective (adj.)Meaning: based on personal feelings, opinions or experiences rather than on facts. Synonyms: personal, biased, one-sided. Example 1: Our evaluation of a movie is often highly subjective. 中文：我们对一部电影的评价往往是非常主观的。 8. tolerant (adj.)Meaning: willing to accept other people’s opinions, behaviors or beliefs, even if you do not agree with them. Synonyms: open-minded, accepting, patient. Example 1: A tolerant society respects different cultures and lifestyles. 中文：一个宽容的社会会尊重不同的文化和生活方式。 Writing vocabulary and phrases for essayssubstantiate (v.)Meaning: to provide facts, evidence or examples to show that a claim or opinion is true. Synonyms: prove, support, confirm. Example: You need to substantiate your argument with clear data and research findings. 中文：你需要用清晰的数据和研究结果来证实你的观点。 exacerbate (v.)Meaning: to make a problem, situation or feeling worse than before. Synonyms: worsen, aggravate, intensify. Example: Cutting public services may exacerbate social inequality. 中文：削减公共服务可能会加剧社会不平等。 advocate (v.n.)Meaning: (v.) to publicly support or recommend a particular idea, policy or way of doing something; (n.) a person who strongly supports something. Synonyms: support, champion, recommend. Example: Many experts advocate taking more effective measures to protect the environment. 中文：许多专家提倡采取更有效的措施来保护环境。 utilize (v.)Meaning: to use something in an effective or practical way. Synonyms: use, employ, make use of. Example: Students should learn to utilize online resources to improve their English. 中文：学生应该学会利用网络资源来提高英语水平。 give rise toFunction in writing: a formal phrase that can often replace “cause” or “lead to” in academic sentences. Example: Rapid urbanization has given rise to a series of environmental problems. 中文：快速城市化引发了一系列环境问题。 be indispensable toFunction in writing: a stronger and more formal way to say “be important to”. Example: Water is indispensable to life. 中文：水对生命来说是不可或缺的。 attach great importance toFunction in writing: can replace verbs like “value” or expressions like “think highly of” in formal writing. Example: The government attaches great importance to education. 中文：政府非常重视教育。 ReflectionToday I moved beyond basic meaning memorization and focused on how these words actually function in serious discussions and formal writing. Words like “ambivalent”, “subjective” and “tolerant” help me describe attitudes more accurately, while verbs like “substantiate” and “exacerbate” make my arguments in essays sound more logical and precise. In the next entries, I will continue to combine core vocabulary with writing-focused expressions, and try to design short review tasks that connect Day 1 and Day 2 so that these words can truly become part of my active vocabulary.

title: CET-6 Vocabulary Journal - Day 1
content: This post kicks off my CET-6 vocabulary journal. Each entry focuses on a small, high-value set of words that appear frequently in academic reading and exams. IntroductionFor Day 1, I selected 12 academic words that often show up in CET-6 passages. For each word I include: a concise definition my own understanding in plain language at least one natural example sentence Word list for Day 11. impartial (adj.)Meaning: fair and not influenced by personal feelings or interests. We need an impartial government to make just and reasonable laws for everyone. Impartial judges are essential to maintaining public trust in the legal system. 2. empirical (adj.)Meaning: based on experience, observation or experiments instead of only on theory. Good scientists rely on empirical evidence instead of personal assumptions. The theory still lacks sufficient empirical support. 3. plausible (adj.)Meaning: seeming reasonable or likely to be true, although it might still be false. Despite his plausible excuse, the teacher still didn’t believe him. The researchers offered a plausible explanation for the unexpected results. 4. mitigate (v.)Meaning: to make something less serious, harmful or painful. The new policy is designed to mitigate the effects of the economic crisis. Taking regular breaks can help mitigate the negative impact of long study sessions. 5. subsidy (n.)Meaning: money given by a government, company or organization to support a person, business or activity. The company offers a housing subsidy to help employees pay their rent. Many students depend on government subsidies to afford higher education. 6. distort (v.)Meaning: to change something so that it is no longer true, clear or accurate. Newspapers are often accused of distorting the truth. Loud background noise can distort the quality of the recording. 7. conform (v.)Meaning: to obey rules, standards or laws, or to behave in the same way as most other people. The kitchen does not conform to hygiene regulations. The changes were introduced to conform with international classifications. 8. hygiene (n.)Meaning: the practice of keeping yourself and your surroundings clean to maintain health and prevent disease. Good personal hygiene, such as washing your hands regularly, can reduce the risk of infection. Poor hygiene conditions in the dormitory may lead to the spread of illness. 9. compliment (n.v.)Meaning: a polite expression of praise or admiration; to say something nice about someone. He paid me a compliment on my presentation. I really appreciate your compliment on my English. Note: this is different from “complement”, which means to make something complete or go well together. 10. contend (v.)Meaning: to argue or claim strongly that something is true. People once contended that the earth was flat, but as people travelled farther, the idea that the earth is round finally won. Many experts contend that regular reading is one of the most effective ways to improve language skills. 11. indifferent (adj.)Meaning: having no strong interest, feeling or opinion about something. She was surprisingly indifferent to the result of the exam. We cannot remain indifferent to environmental problems. 12. compact (adj.)Meaning: smaller than usual or taking up little space compared with similar things. I prefer driving a compact car because it is easier to park in the city. The laptop has a compact design but powerful performance. ReflectionWriting down these words with my own explanations and examples helps me remember them more deeply than just reading a word list. I will keep revisiting today’s vocabulary over the next few days, and in later posts I will add more CET-6 words and short review exercises, so that my active vocabulary grows in a consistent and measurable way.

title: Opencode Antigravity Auth 插件：通过 Google OAuth 解锁 Antigravity 配额
content: Opencode Antigravity Auth 插件：通过 Google OAuth 解锁 Antigravity 配额在现代 AI 辅助开发中，获取稳定且高质量的模型访问至关重要。今天我要介绍一个实用的 Opencode 插件——opencode-antigravity-auth，它能让你通过 Google OAuth 认证来访问 Antigravity（Google 的 IDE）的配额，从而使用 Claude Opus 4.6、Gemini 3.1 Pro 等高级模型。 主要功能这个插件提供了以下核心特性： 多模型支持 - 通过 Google OAuth 访问 Claude Opus 4.6、Sonnet 4.6 和 Gemini 3.1 ProFlash 多账户管理 - 支持添加多个 Google 账户，配额耗尽时自动轮换 双配额系统 - 同时访问 Antigravity 和 Gemini CLI 的配额 思维模型支持 - 支持 Claude 和 Gemini 3 的扩展思维功能，可配置思维预算 Google 搜索接地 - 为 Gemini 模型启用网络搜索功能 自动恢复 - 自动处理会话错误和工具故障 插件兼容性 - 可与其他 OpenCode 插件（oh-my-opencode、dcp 等）配合使用 安装步骤方式一：自动安装（推荐）将以下命令粘贴到任何 LLM 代理（Claude Code、OpenCode、Cursor 等）中： Install the opencode-antigravity-auth plugin and add the Antigravity model definitions to ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json by following: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NoeFabris/opencode-antigravity-auth/dev/README.md 方式二：手动安装 添加插件配置 在 ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json 中添加： plugin: [opencode-antigravity-auth@latest] 登录 Google 账户 opencode auth login 配置模型 运行 opencode auth login → 选择 Google → OAuth with Google (Antigravity) → 选择 “Configure models in opencode.json”（自动配置所有模型） 开始使用 opencode run Hello --model=google/antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking --variant=max 模型参考Antigravity 配额（Claude 和 Gemini 的默认路由） 模型 变体 说明 antigravity-gemini-3-pro low, high Gemini 3 Pro 支持思维 antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro low, high Gemini 3.1 Pro 支持思维（取决于部署情况） antigravity-gemini-3-flash minimal, low, medium, high Gemini 3 Flash 支持思维 antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6 — Claude Sonnet 4.6 antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking low, max Claude Opus 4.6 支持扩展思维 Gemini CLI 配额（与 Antigravity 分开的配额池） 模型 说明 gemini-2.5-flash Gemini 2.5 Flash gemini-2.5-pro Gemini 2.5 Pro gemini-3-flash-preview Gemini 3 Flash（预览版） gemini-3-pro-preview Gemini 3 Pro（预览版） gemini-3.1-pro-preview Gemini 3.1 Pro（预览版） 完整配置示例将以下配置添加到 ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json： $schema: https://opencode.ai/config.json, plugin: [opencode-antigravity-auth@latest], provider: google: models: antigravity-gemini-3-pro: name: Gemini 3 Pro (Antigravity), limit: context: 1048576, output: 65535 , modalities: input: [text, image, pdf], output: [text] , variants: low: thinkingLevel: low , high: thinkingLevel: high , antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking: name: Claude Opus 4.6 Thinking (Antigravity), limit: context: 200000, output: 64000 , modalities: input: [text, image, pdf], output: [text] , variants: low: thinkingConfig: thinkingBudget: 8192 , max: thinkingConfig: thinkingBudget: 32768 // ... 其他模型配置 多账户设置要获得更高的配额，可以添加多个 Google 账户： opencode auth login # 再次运行以添加更多账户 账户管理选项： 配置模型 - 在 opencode.json 中自动配置所有插件模型 检查配额 - 查看每个账户的剩余 API 配额 管理账户 - 启用禁用用于轮换的特定账户 故障排除快速重置大多数问题可以通过删除账户文件并重新登录解决： rm ~/.config/opencode/antigravity-accounts.jsonopencode auth login 常见问题 403 权限拒绝 - 需要在 Google Cloud Console 中启用 Gemini for Google Cloud API 找不到 Gemini 模型 - 在 provider 配置中添加 npm: @ai-sdk/google 400 错误 - 更新到最新测试版或禁用不兼容的 MCP 服务器 所有账户速率限制 - 更新插件或删除账户文件重新认证 重要警告⚠️ 使用风险提示 使用此插件可能违反 Google 的服务条款。一些用户报告了他们的 Google 账户被禁止或影子禁止（无明确通知的限制访问）。 使用此插件即表示你承认： 这是一个未经 Google 认可的非官方工具 你的账户可能被暂停或永久禁止 你需要承担使用此插件的所有风险 项目信息 GitHub 仓库：NoeFabrisopencode-antigravity-auth 许可证：MIT License 支持：如果此插件对你有帮助，可以考虑在 Ko-fi 上支持开发者 这个插件为 Opencode 用户提供了强大的模型访问能力，但使用时请务必了解相关风险。对于需要高质量 AI 辅助开发的开发者来说，这是一个值得尝试的工具。

title: 我的 CET6 词汇学习 App
content: 简介这是一款基于 AI 的 CET6 词汇学习应用，通过改写来自 Global News 的真实文章，将其转化为使用 CET6 词汇的版本，帮助学习者在真实语境中掌握六级核心词汇。 🔗 在线体验: https://hankhills.vercel.app 核心功能1. 文章来源从 Global News 获取最新的真实新闻文章，确保学习内容的时效性和真实性。 2. AI 智能改写利用 AI 技术将原文改写为 CET6 词汇版本： 保留原文核心意思 替换为六级考纲词汇 保持语句通顺自然 3. 对比学习同时展示原文和改写后的版本，方便对比学习： 理解词汇替换的语境 掌握高级词汇的使用场景 技术栈 前端: 部署在 Vercel AI: 智能文本改写 数据源: Global News API 使用建议 先阅读原文，理解大意 对比改写版本，标记不熟悉的 CET6 词汇 尝试用自己的话复述文章 定期复习学过的词汇 总结通过真实新闻文章学习 CET6 词汇，不仅能提高词汇量，还能了解时事，一举两得。欢迎体验！ 项目地址: https://hankhills.vercel.app

title: Hexo 主题 Markdown 全面渲染测试
content: 这是一篇用于测试 Hexo 主题（特别是 Matery 等现代化主题）底层渲染引擎兼容性的标准演示文章。你可以通过本文检验当前主题的字体排版、代码高亮以及公式解析是否正常。 1. 文本排版与强调在日常的技术笔记写作中，我们经常需要强调某些核心概念（加粗文字），或者使用专用学术术语（斜体文字）。如果需要标记已经废弃的电路设计方案，可以使用删除线。 2. 列表排版测试2.1 无序列表（知识点归纳）在复习半导体物理时，我们需要重点掌握以下核心器件结构： PN结二极管 双极型晶体管 (BJT) 金属-氧化物-半导体场效应管 (MOSFET) 2.2 有序列表（操作步骤流程）进行嵌入式微控制器开发的标准流程如下： 配置系统时钟树与 GPIO 引脚映射。 编写底层外设（如 I2CSPI）初始化代码。 编译工程，并使用 SWD 接口烧录至核心板进行联机调试。 3. 代码高亮引擎测试日常记录编程技术方案时，代码块的高亮着色非常重要。 行内代码：我们可以使用 printf() 语句来进行简单的串口数据流格式化输出。 多行代码块（C语言）： #include stdio.h #include stdint.h // 这是一个用于测试高亮渲染的延时函数 void delay(uint32_t count) { while(count--) { // 汇编指令占位，防止被编译器优化 __asm(nop); } } int main() { uint32_t test_val = 1024; printf(Initialization complete. Value: %d , test_val); return 0; }

title: STM32 SPI 通信配置记录
content: 本文记录基于 STM32 实现 SPI 全双工通信的底层寄存器配置流程。 1. GPIO 复用配置在启用 SPI 硬件宏单元前，必须先使能对应端口的时钟，并将引脚配置为复用推挽输出模式。 2. 核心代码实现// SPI1 初始化函数片段 void SPI1_Init(void) { // 假设此处为时钟使能与 GPIO 配置代码 SPI1-CR1 |= 0x0314; // 设置波特率分频，主模式 SPI1-CR1 |= 0x0040; // 使能 SPI 模块 }

title: 关于我
content: 个人简介黄其宏 (Huang Qihong) 中共预备党员 模拟 IC 设计 · 无线电信号处理 · 嵌入式系统架构 核心身份与定位 学术背景：海南大学 · 电子科学与技术（大三） | GPA 3.84.0（全专业 Top 10） 政治面貌：中共预备党员。在学术科研中坚持实事求是、严谨治学的态度，在集体中致力于发挥先锋模范作用，具备较强的责任感与团队协作意识。 研究方向：模拟集成电路（Analog IC）设计、无线电信号处理、嵌入式系统架构 深度项目复盘1. 基于 TPMS 信号与 SDR 技术的车辆轨迹推算研究这是我目前投入精力最多且最具跨学科挑战性的项目，旨在利用非通信专用的车载传感器信号实现低成本、非侵入式的位置推算。 技术架构：Raspberry Pi 4B 作为核心计算节点，结合 RTL-SDR 进行射频前端信号捕获。 核心攻关点： 协议解析：研究 TPMS 传感器（曼彻斯特编码或 FSKASK 调制）协议，利用 OOKFSK 解调从 433MHz315MHz 提取胎压、温度与传感器 ID。 信号处理：针对低频、间歇性信号编写 Python 脚本处理 IQ 数据并做 FFT，利用 RSSI 变化规律进行距离测算。 算法实现：结合车辆动力学模型，利用多传感器 ID 的时序差异进行空间定位建模，初步实现路径粗粒度推算。 工程价值：从物理层射频采集到应用层算法开发的垂直整合能力，体现复杂电磁环境下提取有效特征信息的能力。 RTL-SDR - Python/FFT - Demod/Decode - RSSI/Time - Trajectory Mapping flowchart TD A[RF Capturebr/433/315 MHz TPMS] -- B[RTL-SDR Front-Endbr/采样 IQ 流] B -- C[预处理br/去直流/带通滤波/下采样] C -- D[频域分析br/FFT + 频谱监测] D -- E[解调层br/OOK / FSK / ASK] E -- F[编码解析br/曼彻斯特/时钟恢复] F -- G[协议解析br/Sensor ID/胎压/温度/校验] G -- H[特征提取br/RSSI/到达时间/发送间隔] H -- I[时序关联br/多 ID 关联与滑窗统计] I -- J[建模估计br/动力学约束 + 粗定位] J -- K[轨迹推算输出br/Trajectory Mapping] subgraph System[系统构成] R[RPi 4Bbr/核心计算节点] S[Python Pipelinebr/FFT/解调/解析] T[数据记录br/原始 IQ + 结构化帧] end B -- R R -- S S -- T 2. 全国大学生电子设计竞赛 (TI 杯) - 省一等奖项目 工作重点：高精度模拟前端电路设计与调试。 成果：解决微弱信号噪声抑制问题，优化 STM32 与底层外设通信效率，作品在功耗与测量精度上达到竞赛顶尖水平，获省级一等奖。 3. 集成电路设计（集创赛在研） 领域：模拟 IC 设计。 实践：使用 Cadence Virtuoso 完成 OP-AMP 设计、ACDC 仿真、噪声分析与 Layout 绘制，完成 DRCLVS 验证。 技术技能栈 EDA 开发工具：Cadence Virtuoso, MATLABSimulink, HBuilderX (uni-app), Keil MDK, STM32CubeIDE 硬件描述与编程：Verilog HDL、C 语言（指针、结构体与内存管理）、Python（数据处理与信号模拟） 理论体系：半导体物理（MOSFETPN 结机理）、信号与系统（FourierZ 变换）、电子线路（低频高频） 综合素养与个人特质 教学经验：曾受聘担任《相对论与量子力学入门》课程助教，善于将复杂学术概念转化为易懂逻辑。 自我驱动：坚持规律的“三分化”健身训练，具备较强抗压能力与时间管理能力。 工程审美：追求经得起检验的代码与电路设计，推崇客观、科学、用事实说话的工作准则。 交流与合作我正在寻找模拟集成电路或高阶嵌入式系统方向的科研实习机会。如果你对我的 TPMS 项目或技术栈感兴趣，欢迎联系我。 技术标签：#模拟IC #嵌入式 #SDR #预备党员 #海南大学

title: Today
content: Today's Sentence (function () { const quotes = [ { zh: '愿你在平凡的日子里，也能被温柔的光轻轻照亮，像春天一样安静，却始终明亮。', en: 'May even your ordinary days be gently lit by tender light, quiet like spring, yet always bright.' }, { zh: '愿你的心像清晨的湖面，安静、澄澈，偶尔起风，也总能回到温柔。', en: 'May your heart be like a lake at dawn: calm, clear, and always finding its way back to softness.' }, { zh: '把昨日放在身后，把明天轻轻放远，今天只负责认真地呼吸和热爱。', en: 'Leave yesterday behind, let tomorrow stay distant, and let today be for breathing deeply and loving well.' }, { zh: '愿你走过的路都开着花，哪怕只是无人留意的角落，也有自己的春天。', en: 'May the paths you walk bloom with flowers, and even the unseen corners of the world hold a spring of their own.' }, { zh: '在漫长的岁月里，愿你始终保有一份不慌不忙的从容。', en: 'Through the long years, may you always keep a calm that does not hurry.' }, { zh: '愿那些温柔而坚定的瞬间，慢慢把你的一生照亮。', en: 'May the gentle yet steadfast moments slowly light up your whole life.' }, { zh: '把心交给晨光，把脚步交给远方，把自己交给值得的人与事。', en: 'Give your heart to the morning light, your steps to the distant road, and yourself to what is truly worth it.' }, { zh: '愿你偶尔疲惫时，也能被一阵风、一朵云、一句话轻轻安慰。', en: 'When you are tired, may a breeze, a cloud, or a single sentence comfort you gently.' }, { zh: '愿你不必成为最耀眼的人，也依然能做最安稳的自己。', en: 'May you not need to be the brightest person to remain your most steady self.' }, { zh: '有些答案不必急着知道，时间会把最合适的光递到你手里。', en: 'Some answers need not be known at once; time will place the right light in your hands.' } ]; const todayKey = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10); const cacheKey = 'today-quote:' + todayKey; const cached = localStorage.getItem(cacheKey); let current; if (cached) { current = JSON.parse(cached); } else { const daySeed = Number(todayKey.replace(/-/g, '')); current = quotes[daySeed % quotes.length]; localStorage.setItem(cacheKey, JSON.stringify(current)); } document.getElementById('today-zh').textContent = '中文：' + current.zh; document.getElementById('today-en').textContent = 'English: ' + current.en; })();
